articles.title: Trump’s Foreign Policy: Resurrecting Empire
articles.summary: The article argues that Trump's foreign policy approach represents a revival of imperial ambitions, focused on acquiring territories and resources of sovereign peoples rather than pursuing traditional diplomatic goals.

articles.title: Fact-check: In Trump’s Case for an Attack on Iran, False or Unproven Claims
articles.summary: The article fact-checks the Trump administration's justifications for a potential military campaign against Iran, finding that key claims are either false or unproven.

articles.title: Vance Continues to Criticize Democrats’ Attitude During State of the Union
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance, speaking in Wisconsin ahead of midterm elections, criticized Democrats for not standing during parts of Trump's State of the Union address, continuing a line of political attack.

articles.title: Judge Says U.S. Is Intentionally Violating the Law on Immigration
articles.summary: Federal Judge Zahid N. Quraishi accused the U.S. government of intentionally violating immigration law, saying federal prosecutors in New Jersey had lost credibility. He is among several judges showing impatience with the Trump administration's immigration enforcement.

articles.title: Justice Department Sues 5 States, Most Republican-Led, for Voter Data
articles.summary: The Trump Justice Department sued five states—Utah, Oklahoma, West Virginia, Kentucky, and New Jersey—to obtain unredacted voter registration databases as part of its pursuit of unsubstantiated claims of voter fraud.

articles.title: Anthropic Says It Cannot ‘Accede’ to Pentagon in Talks Over A.I.
articles.summary: AI company Anthropic said it cannot comply with Pentagon demands for unfettered access to its artificial intelligence technology, standing firm on restrictions against certain military uses as a Pentagon-imposed deadline approaches.

articles.title: Regime Change in Cuba Appeals to Trump but Carries Risks
articles.summary: The article examines the Trump administration's signaling toward regime change in Cuba, including demands to end communist leadership, while noting the significant risks such an approach carries.

articles.title: Hillary Clinton Denies Knowing Epstein or His Crimes in a Tense Deposition
articles.summary: Hillary Clinton testified in a deposition denying knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein or his crimes, growing irate after a Republican leaked a photo from the session. The article does not involve the Supreme Court or any pending cases before it.

articles.title: Mamdani Meets Again With Trump, Emerging With Two Unexpected Victories
articles.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani met with President Trump to discuss housing development and appeared to secure the release of a Columbia student detained by ICE. The article does not relate to any Supreme Court proceedings.

articles.title: Trump’s Shields Are Down
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that Trump's personnel choices are now driven by flattery rather than policy competence, weakening his administration's effectiveness. It does not discuss the Supreme Court or any specific cases.

articles.title: Donald Trump Is the Crypto President. Why Is It Struggling?
articles.summary: The article examines why the cryptocurrency industry is struggling despite Trump's pro-crypto stance, with the authors arguing that crypto's core product remains basically useless. It does not involve the Supreme Court.

articles.title: Fed seeks to block Justice Dept. subpoenas in Powell probe
articles.summary: The Federal Reserve is seeking to block Justice Department subpoenas issued as part of an investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell, asking a court to quash them. The article does not directly discuss a current Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Hillary Clinton derides her Epstein deposition as GOP ‘political theater’
articles.summary: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton appeared for a deposition before the Republican-led House Oversight Committee regarding Jeffrey Epstein, dismissing the proceedings as political theater. The article does not discuss any Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Senator seeks to block Trump’s NSA pick, citing civil liberties concerns
articles.summary: Senator Ron Wyden is attempting to block Trump's nominee to lead the NSA, citing civil liberties concerns and calling the pick unqualified. The article does not directly relate to any pending Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Economic nationalism is just getting started
articles.summary: The article argues that economic nationalism and protectionist trade policies are intensifying globally, with companies increasingly expected to prioritize national interests. It does not reference any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: Paramount clinches Warner Bros deal after Netflix walks away
articles.summary: Paramount has reached a deal to acquire Warner Bros after Netflix withdrew from the bidding, ending a months-long battle over the parent company of CNN, HBO, and major entertainment franchises. The article is about corporate media deals and does not involve any Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Pentagon moves to build AI tools for China cyber operations
articles.summary: The Pentagon is working with AI companies to develop tools that can identify vulnerabilities in Chinese networks and support U.S. cyber operations and war planning. The article focuses on national security and defense technology, not any Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Court rejects ICE contractor’s right to immediate appeal
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled in GEO Group v. Menocal that an ICE contractor cannot pursue an immediate appeal of a district court judgment. The decision limits the procedural options available to private contractors working with immigration enforcement agencies.

articles.title: Beach blasts and unusually dangerous weapons
articles.summary: SCOTUSblog's Relist Watch column examines cert petitions that the Supreme Court has relisted for an upcoming conference, focusing on cases involving beach blasts and unusually dangerous weapons. The column tracks petitions the justices are giving additional consideration before deciding whether to grant review.

articles.title: Trump administration asks justices to allow it to remove protected status from Syrian nationals
articles.summary: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court to stay a federal judge's ruling that indefinitely postponed the termination of Temporary Protected Status for Syrian nationals. The administration seeks to end the program that allows Syrians to remain in the U.S. and is asking the justices to allow the termination to proceed.

articles.title: Court to hear argument on whether and when drug users may possess firearms
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will hear oral arguments in United States v. Hemani, which addresses whether and when drug users may possess firearms. This is the second gun-rights case of the 2025-26 term, following a January case involving Hawaii gun laws.

articles.title: Court rules criminal defendants may be prohibited from discussing ongoing testimony with counsel during an overnight recess
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that trial courts may prohibit criminal defendants from discussing their ongoing testimony with their lawyers during an overnight recess. The decision addresses the balance between a defendant's right to counsel and the court's interest in ensuring the integrity of testimony.

articles.title: How can the Supreme Court protect electoral integrity?
articles.summary: This article, part of an election law series by Edward B. Foley, discusses how the Supreme Court can protect electoral integrity. It notes the Court has already confronted cases related to elections and explores what further steps may be appropriate.

articles.title: SCOTUStoday for Thursday, February 26
articles.summary: The daily SCOTUStoday roundup reports that a new poll found 57% of Americans approve of the Supreme Court's tariffs ruling while 23% disapprove. The article provides a digest of the day's Supreme Court-related news and coverage.

articles.title: Anthropic Takes a Stand
articles.summary: This article discusses Anthropic's refusal to comply with Pentagon demands. It does not relate to any Supreme Court cases or proceedings.

articles.title: The First Couple of a Dysfunctional DHS
articles.summary: A forthcoming book reveals new details about Kristi Noem and Corey Lewandowski's roles at the Department of Homeland Security. The article does not discuss any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: The Degraded State of the Union
articles.summary: This article discusses the declining quality of presidential oratory, noting that State of the Union addresses once aimed to elevate audiences through serious and artful rhetoric. It does not relate to Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: Today’s <em>Atlantic </em>Trivia: Name That Norman Rockwell Subject
articles.summary: This is a trivia feature from The Atlantic about Norman Rockwell subjects. It has no connection to the Supreme Court or its cases.

articles.title: Trump’s Favorite Voter-ID Bill Would Probably Backfire
articles.summary: The article examines a strict voter-ID bill favored by Trump and congressional Republicans, arguing it could paradoxically disenfranchise Republican voters. It does not directly discuss any pending Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: The Tariffs Loss Is Paradoxically a Win for Trump
articles.summary: The article argues that the Supreme Court's ruling against Trump on tariffs paradoxically benefits the president by preserving the Court's perceived legitimacy for when it rules in his favor on other parts of his agenda. It discusses the broader strategic implications of the tariffs decision.

articles.title: The Media Merger You Should Actually Care About
articles.summary: The article examines a Trump-approved media merger that could create a massive broadcasting company controlling local news stations in over forty states, and explores why some MAGA supporters oppose the deal.

articles.title: The Hidden History of Native American Enslavement
articles.summary: The article discusses the hidden history of Native American enslavement, which lasted for centuries under many names, and highlights a new public history project aimed at bringing awareness to this overlooked chapter of American history.

articles.title: Trump’s Go-To Tactic in the State of the Union
articles.summary: The article analyzes a moment in Trump's State of the Union address where he deployed a familiar tactic on immigration, examining the broader context of his rhetoric on the issue.

articles.title: Trump Declared Victory in Minneapolis. But What Did He Accomplish?
articles.summary: The article examines a Trump administration law enforcement operation in Minneapolis that turned lethal and politically controversial, suggesting the show of force may have been intended as a broader warning rather than a targeted accomplishment.

articles.title: Government Still Building Case for U.S.S. Cole Trial 25 Years After Attack
articles.summary: Twenty-five years after the U.S.S. Cole attack, prosecutors are still building their case while dealing with adverse judicial rulings, and defense lawyers say they are not prepared for trial.

articles.title: In Texas, Crenshaw faces primary challenger, a test for MAGA
articles.summary: This article covers a Republican primary challenge against Representative Dan Crenshaw in Texas, focusing on intra-party dynamics between MAGA-aligned conservatives and more moderate Republicans. It does not discuss the Supreme Court or any pending cases.

articles.title: Trump Administration Asks Supreme Court to End Protections for Syrian Migrants
articles.summary: The Trump administration has asked the Supreme Court to end Temporary Protected Status protections for Syrian migrants as part of its broader mass deportation agenda. This legal action seeks to override lower court rulings that had maintained protections for these migrants.

articles.title: Judge Declines to Halt Construction on Trump’s East Wing Ballroom Project
articles.summary: A federal judge declined to halt construction on Trump's East Wing ballroom project, ruling the lawsuit challenging it needed to be revised before the court could address the larger legal questions. This involves a lower federal court, not the Supreme Court.

articles.title: Justice Gorsuch’s Tariffs Warning
articles.summary: Justice Neil Gorsuch issued a warning about the dangers of granting broad tariff powers to the president, noting that once new executive power is granted, it is nearly impossible to reclaim. His comments reflect broader concerns about the separation of powers and congressional delegation of authority to the executive branch.

articles.title: New A.C.A. Plans Could Increase Family Deductibles to $31,000
articles.summary: The Trump administration is proposing new Affordable Care Act plans that could raise family deductibles to $31,000 while promising lower premiums. Critics warn the changes would make actual healthcare unaffordable for many families. The article does not discuss Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders’s Plan to Build a Prison in Arkansas Divides GOP
articles.summary: Arkansas Governor Sarah Huckabee Sanders is pushing to build a large, expensive prison to house inmates incarcerated under her tough-on-crime policies, creating divisions within the state Republican Party. The article does not involve the Supreme Court.

articles.title: First the Moon, Then Mars
articles.summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses how his personal space travel experiences shaped his views on the future of lunar and Mars exploration in an interview with columnist Ross Douthat. The article does not relate to the Supreme Court.

articles.title: The Incompetence of Trump 2.0
articles.summary: Times Opinion editor Kathleen Kingsbury argues that carelessness and incompetence among Trump's top officials are putting Americans at risk, contrary to their image as cunning political strategists. The article does not discuss the Supreme Court.

articles.title: Trump’s State of the Union Was Rage Bait
articles.summary: Columnist Jamelle Bouie argues that Trump's State of the Union address relied on rage bait to distract from his failure to govern effectively. The article does not involve any Supreme Court cases or proceedings.

articles.title: The Trumpiest Trump We’ve Ever Beheld
articles.summary: This opinion piece contends that President Trump is becoming a reality-denying machine, mirroring the tendencies that the author argues destroyed the Democratic Party. It does not discuss the Supreme Court.

articles.title: I’m a Mathematician. Trump’s Fun With Numbers Is Embarrassing.
articles.summary: A mathematician critiques the Trump administration's creative and misleading use of numbers and statistics in public communications. The article does not relate to the Supreme Court or any pending cases.

articles.title: Trump’s State of the Union Was a Win for Democrats
articles.summary: Ezra Klein and Aaron Retica argue that Trump's 2026 State of the Union speech was ultimately beneficial for Democrats. The article does not discuss the Supreme Court.

articles.title: These Iranian Doctors Risked Their Lives So You Could See These Images
articles.summary: The article highlights the risks taken by Iranian doctors who smuggled evidence of the regime's violent crackdown on protesters out of the country. It does not discuss the Supreme Court or any related cases.

articles.title: On Youth Gender Care, Medical Associations Hurt Their Credibility
articles.summary: This opinion piece critiques major medical associations for undermining their own credibility on youth gender care by not fully engaging with evolving scientific evidence. While it touches on themes related to transgender rights cases before the Court, it does not directly discuss a specific SCOTUS case.

articles.title: Vance: ‘No chance’ U.S. will be in drawn-out war in Middle East
articles.summary: Vice President Vance told The Washington Post that despite Trump's contemplation of military action against Iran, there is no chance the U.S. will become involved in a prolonged Middle East conflict. The article does not discuss any Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Kash Patel purges more FBI agents tied to Mar-a-Lago investigation
articles.summary: FBI Director Kash Patel has dismissed additional FBI agents and staff connected to the investigation into Trump's handling of classified documents at Mar-a-Lago. The article does not directly discuss a current Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Prosecutor says Trump officials did not pressure him to charge Abrego García
articles.summary: A federal prosecutor told a judge that Trump administration officials did not pressure him to bring charges against Kilmar Abrego García, a Maryland man who had successfully challenged his deportation to El Salvador. The case relates to the broader legal battle over the administration's deportation actions that have been the subject of Supreme Court proceedings.

articles.title: Man pleads guilty in death threat to Kennedy Center’s Richard Grenell
articles.summary: A Virginia man pleaded guilty in federal court to making death threats against Kennedy Center President Richard Grenell and others. The article does not discuss any Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Trump, seeking executive power over elections, is urged to declare emergency
articles.summary: Activists reportedly coordinating with the White House are circulating a draft executive order that would give the president extraordinary emergency powers over elections and voting. The article does not directly discuss a specific Supreme Court case.

articles.title: DOJ reviewing if Epstein files with Trump allegations were wrongly withheld
articles.summary: The Justice Department is reviewing whether FBI interview summaries involving allegations against Trump related to the Epstein investigation were improperly withheld from the released files. The article does not discuss a Supreme Court case.

articles.title: The Epstein files have brought a wave of resignations and investigations
articles.summary: The release of Epstein files has triggered a wave of resignations and investigations involving prominent figures whose communications with Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell have come to light. The article does not discuss any Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Judge rules Trump’s White House ballroom can continue, for now
articles.summary: A federal judge ruled that construction of Trump's planned $400 million White House ballroom project can continue for now, though challengers said they would immediately seek to stop it again. The article does not directly discuss a current Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
articles.summary: This article tracks President Trump's progress in filling over 800 key administration positions that require Senate confirmation. It is a general executive branch staffing tracker and does not discuss any specific Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Trump stirred rumors for years. Now, it’s the Clintons’ turn to speak about Epstein.
articles.summary: The article covers Bill and Hillary Clinton responding to long-running rumors and conspiracy theories related to Jeffrey Epstein. It does not involve or discuss any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: Republicans think they laid an immigration trap ahead of the midterms
articles.summary: Republicans are positioning immigration as a key issue ahead of the midterm elections, even as polling shows their traditional advantage on the topic is eroding. The article focuses on political strategy and does not discuss any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: Trump administration to withhold $259M in Minnesota Medicaid funds, citing fraud
articles.summary: The Trump administration announced it will withhold $259 million in Minnesota Medicaid funds over alleged health care fraud, with Vice President Vance signaling a broader crackdown on other states. The article focuses on executive branch enforcement and does not reference any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: The Alito Vacancy
articles.summary: This article discusses a potential vacancy on the Supreme Court following Justice Alito's anticipated departure, weighing various scenarios for how the seat might be filled. While it directly concerns the Supreme Court, it does not discuss any specific pending case from the current term.

articles.title: The Next ‘Cold War’: Competition Over Arctic Resources
articles.summary: The article examines geopolitical competition over Arctic energy and mineral resources, focusing on what the U.S. needs to do to secure access. It does not discuss any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: Fall in UK net migration threatens to carve deep hole in public finances
articles.summary: Falling net migration in the UK is expected to create a significant gap in public finances, an issue likely to feature in Chancellor Rachel Reeves' upcoming Spring Statement. This is a UK fiscal policy article with no relevance to the U.S. Supreme Court.

articles.title: Justices send litigation about tainted baby food back to state court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court decided Hain Celestial Group v. Palmquist, sending litigation about tainted baby food back to state court. The ruling resolves a technical jurisdictional question about what happens when a district court erroneously rules on its own jurisdiction.

articles.title: Justices reveal little about whether the deadline for removing cases to federal court can be excused
articles.summary: The Supreme Court heard arguments but revealed little about whether the 30-day deadline for defendants to remove cases from state court to federal court can be excused. The case concerns the procedural rules governing when federal jurisdiction can be invoked after a lawsuit is filed in state court.

articles.title: Opinions for Wednesday, February 25
articles.summary: The Supreme Court released opinions in two cases on Wednesday, February 25: Villarreal v. Texas and The GEO Group, Inc. v. Menocal. SCOTUSblog provided live coverage as both decisions were handed down.

articles.title: How America Chose Not to Hold the Powerful to Account
articles.summary: This article discusses America's broader pattern of failing to hold powerful people accountable, describing a national project of elite impunity. It does not directly discuss any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: The Democrats Who Got Weird During the State of the Union
articles.summary: The article covers Democratic lawmakers' unusual and crass behavior during Trump's State of the Union address as a form of protest. It does not discuss any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: When Caring Becomes Counterculture
articles.summary: This article features commentary on political culture including observations from Minnesota, generational attitudes toward resistance, and reactions to Trump's tariff defeat and the State of the Union. It does not directly discuss Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: Lucky Winner Mike Johnson Gets Dream Trip to State of the Union
articles.summary: The article humorously describes House Speaker Mike Johnson's demeanor during the State of the Union address alongside President Trump. It does not relate to any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: The State of the Union Revealed a Sad Reality
articles.summary: The article critiques Donald Trump's State of the Union address, arguing he misused the annual presidential tradition in unprecedented and radical ways that call into question the value of the ritual itself.

articles.title: Donald Trump’s State of the Union Was Long and Wrong
articles.summary: The article provides critical commentary on Donald Trump's State of the Union address, characterizing it as excessively long and filled with inaccuracies, while noting the President's self-congratulatory tone.

articles.title: How Did Trump’s State of the Union Play?
articles.summary: Readers respond to Trump's State of the Union address with mostly critical reactions. The article does not involve the Supreme Court or any specific cases.

articles.title: What Hath Trump Wrought
articles.summary: This opinion piece reflects on the real state of the union under Trump's leadership. It does not discuss the Supreme Court or any pending cases.

articles.title: Best and Worst Moments From Trump’s State of the Union Address
articles.summary: Eight opinion writers highlight the best and worst moments from Trump's State of the Union address. The article does not discuss the Supreme Court or any related cases.

articles.title: John Roberts Is Losing Patience With Trump
articles.summary: An opinion piece analyzes Chief Justice John Roberts's opinion in a Supreme Court ruling that struck down Trump's tariffs, highlighting a pointed paragraph that suggests Roberts is growing frustrated with the Trump administration's repeated legal overreach. The piece interprets the language as a signal that the Court may take an increasingly firm stance against executive power grabs.

articles.title: Rep. Ilhan Omar demands investigation after State of the Union guest arrested
articles.summary: Rep. Ilhan Omar is demanding an investigation after her guest, Aliya Rahman, was arrested and allegedly mistreated at the State of the Union address. The article does not discuss any Supreme Court proceedings or cases.

articles.title: Republicans stare down epic voter enthusiasm gap ahead of 2026 midterms
articles.summary: Democrats hold a 14-percentage-point voter enthusiasm advantage over Republicans heading into the 2026 midterms, the largest such gap in decades. This is a political polling and elections article with no connection to any Supreme Court case.

articles.title: Trump moves to remake Dulles Airport, weighing redesign proposals
articles.summary: The Trump administration is considering proposals to redesign Dulles Airport, potentially including a new terminal and removal of the people movers. The article concerns infrastructure planning and does not involve any Supreme Court matters.

articles.title: Trump reframes America, in two acts
articles.summary: The article analyzes President Trump's speech in which he attempted to reframe the national mood despite polls showing widespread public dissatisfaction. It is a political commentary piece and does not discuss any Supreme Court cases.

articles.title: Trump’s New Tariffs Are Illegal Too
articles.summary: This opinion article argues that Trump's latest tariff measures are illegal, following a Supreme Court ruling that struck down earlier tariffs. The author contends the administration is once again flouting the law and daring the judiciary to intervene.

articles.title: The Donald Trump 1 Percent Fan Club
articles.summary: The article discusses Trump's relationship with certain elites who continue to support him, framing them as a small but influential group. It does not directly discuss any specific Supreme Court case.

articles.title: After a Big Loss, What to Expect From Trump at the State of the Union
articles.summary: This opinion piece previews Trump's upcoming State of the Union address in the wake of the Supreme Court's ruling striking down his tariffs, predicting a tense atmosphere. The article explores how the legal defeat may shape the president's rhetoric and political posture.

articles.title: Trump Is Turning the F.C.C. and F.T.C. Against Free Expression
articles.summary: The article argues that Trump appointees at the FCC and FTC are using the agencies to suppress free expression and enforce ideological conformity. It does not directly discuss a specific pending Supreme Court case, though it touches on themes related to federal communications regulation.

articles.title: Kyiv Is Freezing, but There Is Warmth, Too
articles.summary: The article is a personal essay about life in Kyiv amid the ongoing war, contrasting the city's freezing conditions with the warmth of its residents. It does not discuss any Supreme Court matters.

articles.title: Jesse Jackson’s Timeless Economic Platform
articles.summary: The article revisits Jesse Jackson's two presidential campaigns and argues that his economic platform—focused on inequality, affordability, and job loss—remains deeply relevant to contemporary American politics.

articles.title: “Slender Offering,” by Lucie Brock-Broido
articles.summary: This is a poetry publication featuring the poem 'Slender Offering' by Lucie Brock-Broido in The New Yorker. It does not discuss the Supreme Court or any legal matters.

articles.title: The Endless Stages of Enlightenment
articles.summary: This is a light personal essay about moments of everyday enlightenment, including a humorous misunderstanding about football terminology. It does not relate to the Supreme Court.

articles.title: Jan Staller, Constructor of Image
articles.summary: The article profiles photographer Jan Staller, who captures construction sites, and notes his latest subject is the Gateway tunnel project that has been targeted by the Trump administration.

articles.title: The Supreme Court’s Complicated Takedown of Trump’s Tariffs
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued a complex ruling against Trump's tariffs, producing seven separate opinions. Even Justices who reached the same conclusion disagreed on the reasoning, highlighting deep divisions on the Court regarding executive trade authority.

articles.title: One Vaccine-Schedule Change That Actually Makes Sense
articles.summary: Amid criticism of RFK Jr.'s impact on public health, the article highlights a positive development: a vaccine-schedule change related to a cancer-preventing vaccine that experts say is scientifically sound.

articles.title: Assessing the Supreme Court’s Tariff Ruling
articles.summary: This letters-to-the-editor piece features reader reactions to the Supreme Court's ruling rejecting Trump's tariffs, discussing the significance of the decision for executive power and trade policy. Readers weigh in on the implications of the justices' reasoning.

articles.title: ‘A Cocked Pistol Aimed at Iran’
articles.summary: Columnist David French discusses the dangers of Trump's aggressive military posture toward Iran, describing it as a reckless gamble with military power. The article does not directly discuss any Supreme Court case.
